cases.prompt: create a bug report form with required fields
stop blank issues and route questions to discussions
set up good first issue and help wanted labels
our bug reports are useless - make reporters fill in repro steps
why isn't my issue form showing up on github?
triage these open issues for me
require pull requests before merging to main
set up issue templates for this repo
expected_behavior: creates .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/bug_report.yml and feature_request.yml as YAML issue forms (not legacy markdown), with required fields and auto-applied labels
adds config.yml with blank_issues_disabled and contact_links routing questions/security away from issues
uses valid schema (body element types, validations.required) and validates with check-jsonschema --builtin-schema vendor.github-issue-forms
labels referenced by the forms actually exist or are created via gh label
creates a single .github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md with an actionable checklist (tests run, docs updated, linked issue)
keeps it short enough that contributors actually fill it in
if multiple templates are requested, explains GitHub has no picker UI and shows the ?template= workaround

cases.prompt: why isn't my issue form showing?
expected_behavior: checks file location (.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/), .yml extension, presence on the default branch, and schema validity in that order
runs or recommends check-jsonschema to pinpoint the schema error
explains forms only render for the default branch

cases.prompt: make reporters include their version and repro steps
expected_behavior: uses input/textarea elements with validations.required: true
uses render: shell (or similar) for log/output fields so formatting survives
keeps optional fields optional - required-everything forms deter reports
